sector_snapshot.name: India Information Technology
sector_snapshot.stage_detail: Cyclical correction + structural AI transition; near 10-yr valuation lows on P/E

metrics.label: IT exports FY25
metrics.note: +4.6% YoY · 43–45% of India's total services exports

tailwinds: Enterprise AI and GenAI transformation mandates
Cloud migration — multi-cloud and FinOps spending
GCC (Global Capability Centre) expansion in India
Engineering R&D segment growing at 7% — fastest sub-segment
BFSI core banking and payments modernization
Domestic India digital infrastructure spend (+11% YoY)
Rupee depreciation tailwind on USD revenues
NASSCOM: 77% of providers expect higher FY26 business growth
82% of enterprise CXOs plan to increase digital spend 5%+ in CY25
India AI market projected at $28.8B by 2025 at 45% CAGR
risks: AI structural disruption to managed services business mix
US macro slowdown — Fed GDP forecast cut to 1.4% for FY25
Discretionary tech spend freeze by US clients
FII outflows — 26.1% net outflow from IT sector in 2025
Jefferies and JM Financial broad sector downgrades (Feb 2026)
Valuation de-rating from premium 27–35x P/E to historical avg 19–22x
Talent cost pressure and attrition in AI-adjacent roles
Revenue headwinds from AI-driven productivity (deflationary on billings)

entry.rationale: Currently inside zone — accumulate in tranches; trading near 10-yr avg P/E of 22x

thesis.core_business: India's largest IT services firm. IT services, consulting, and BPO across 50+ countries. Revenue diversified across BFSI, retail, manufacturing, telecom, healthcare.
thesis.competitive_advantage: Unmatched scale, brand, and client stickiness. Tata Group governance credibility. Zero meaningful debt. Consistent dividend payer. 600,000+ employees — deepest delivery bench in India.
growth_drivers: TCS GenAI platform — AI-augmented delivery
Large deal ramp-up from BFSI and manufacturing verticals
Domestic GCC partnership business expansion
AI services revenue expected to accelerate H2 2026
risks: Revenue growth sluggish — CC growth 0.3–0.5% QoQ
US macro slowdown suppressing discretionary tech spend
AI productivity gains deflationary on managed services billings
Premium valuation vs global peers (17x vs Accenture 14x)

companies.name: Infosys
companies.category: Large-Cap IT Services — AI & Digital Transformation

entry.rationale: Currently inside zone — trading at 22x FY26E vs 3-yr avg 27x; best large-cap recovery candidate

thesis.core_business: India's second-largest IT company. AI-led digital transformation, cloud migration, and IT consulting. Strong presence in BFSI, manufacturing, energy, and retail across 50+ countries.
thesis.competitive_advantage: Superior management track record. Infosys Cobalt cloud platform. Best-in-class deal pipeline momentum. AI-first delivery model being embedded natively across all verticals.
growth_drivers: Largest deal pipeline in several years
AI transformation mandates from global BFSI clients
Infosys Cobalt cloud acceleration
Strong operational execution lifting revenue guidance
risks: Jefferies downgrade to Hold — AI disruption to managed services
US client budget freeze on discretionary spend
High US revenue concentration (~60% of total)
Revenue growth guidance conservative for FY26

companies.name: HCL Technologies
companies.category: Large-Cap IT Services + ER&D + Software Products

entry.rationale: Best risk-adjusted large-cap entry — ER&D + software product mix protects margins; 19x FY26E is undemanding vs quality

thesis.core_business: Third-largest Indian IT firm. Unique business mix: IT services, engineering R&D, and HCLSoftware product portfolio with annuity-like license renewals. Serves BFSI, hi-tech, and manufacturing globally.
thesis.competitive_advantage: Software product business provides high-margin recurring revenue — a structural differentiator vs pure-play services peers. ER&D exposure to fastest-growing sub-segment. Leading large-cap QoQ growth in Q3FY26.
growth_drivers: HCLSoftware auto-renewing license portfolio (high margins)
ER&D fastest-growing sub-segment globally at 7% CAGR
BFSI and hi-tech vertical momentum
AI-enabled productivity gains boosting margins
risks: Auto manufacturing vertical headwinds
Software business renewal rate monitoring
Jefferies downgrade to Hold citing AI structural risk
US macro exposure through BFSI clients

companies.name: Persistent Systems
companies.category: Mid-Cap IT — Software Product Engineering & AI-Native

financials.revenue_mix_top_vertical: BFSI, Healthcare, Hi-Tech (~85% from North America)
financials.q3_fy26_cc_growth_outperformance: Tier-2 segment outperformer per Emkay Global

price_data.note: JM Financial cut target 21.6% from prior ₹7,600 — reflects sector-wide de-rating, not company deterioration

entry.rationale: Near lower end of zone — wait for pullback to ₹4,200–4,400 for better margin of safety; correction from ₹6,700 peak is opportunity, not signal

thesis.core_business: India's highest-quality mid-cap IT. Focused on software product engineering, cloud-native development, and AI-native application development. Deep partnerships with Microsoft, AWS, Salesforce, and ServiceNow ecosystems.
thesis.competitive_advantage: Fastest organic revenue growth in listed Indian IT (18–21% CAGR FY23–25). AI-first delivery model natively embedded. CEO Sandeep Kalra's credible execution track record. Margin discipline despite growth investments.
growth_drivers: Software product engineering — fastest-growing global sub-segment
AI-native application development mandates
Strong hyperscaler partner ecosystem (AWS, Azure, GCP, Salesforce)
BFSI and healthcare verticals — digital transformation pipeline
risks: Premium valuation still at 30x requires sustained 18%+ growth
North America BFSI concentration — macro sensitivity
JM Financial downgraded; target cut 21.6% in Feb 2026
Stock fell ~31% from peak — further selling pressure possible

price_data.note: JM Financial cut target 31% (prior ₹2,180) — sector-wide de-rating, not company-specific

entry.rationale: Currently inside zone — de-rated sharply from peak; cheapest mid-cap at 17x FY27E; accumulate in tranches

thesis.core_business: Mid-cap IT services with deep vertical specialization in travel, BFSI, and insurance. Following NIIT Technologies merger, now a focused domain-led player with strong deal win rates vs generic peers.
thesis.competitive_advantage: Vertical specialization drives higher win rates and pricing power vs generalist IT. Insurance and travel digitization remain structurally underpenetrated. CEO Sudhir Singh — strong capital allocation and execution record.
growth_drivers: Large deal pipeline in insurance digital transformation
BFSI modernization mandates across US and UK
Geographic expansion into Europe
Travel sector digitization long runway post-pandemic reset
risks: Travel vertical cyclicality — vulnerable to global macro shocks
Stock fell 23% in one month — ongoing sector sentiment pressure
JM Financial downgraded to Add (31% target cut, Feb 2026)
Client concentration risk in travel segment

companies.name: LTIMindtree
companies.category: Large Mid-Cap IT Services — Post-Merger Entity

entry.rationale: Wait for 8–12% correction before entry — merger integration still playing out; execution needs to stabilize for 1–2 more quarters

thesis.core_business: Merged entity of LTI and Mindtree. Large mid-cap IT services player across BFSI, hi-tech, and manufacturing. Combining LTI's BFSI strength with Mindtree's consumer and retail tech capabilities.
thesis.competitive_advantage: Scale post-merger provides large deal eligibility. BFSI domain depth from LTI heritage. Strong talent bench from both organizations. Good balance sheet with low leverage.
growth_drivers: Post-merger synergy realization from FY27 onward
Large deal pipeline benefiting from combined scale
BFSI client wallet share expansion
AI and cloud transformation mandates
risks: Post-merger integration execution risk — still ongoing
Jefferies — Underperform rating, ₹4,300 target (below market)
JM Financial — Reduce rating with ₹5,095 target
Stock fell ~20% in one month; consensus divided

price_data.note: JM Financial — no catalyst for re-rating despite reasonable valuation

entry.rationale: Only on visible management-driven growth inflection — chronically underperforms peers for 3+ years; watchlist until execution improves

thesis.core_business: India's fourth-largest IT services company. Provides IT, consulting, BPO, and cloud services. Undergoing strategic restructuring under CEO Srini Pallia with focus on AI-led delivery (WINGS and WEGA platforms).
thesis.competitive_advantage: Strong balance sheet and dividend payout (~$1.3B annually). Best EBIT margin performance in years at 17.6% Q3FY26. Conservative financial management. But growth persistently lags all large-cap peers.
growth_drivers: WINGS AI delivery platform and WEGA AI innovation network
Phoenix deal ramp-up and vendor consolidation with US banks
AI-led productivity margin expansion
Interim dividend ₹6/share declared Jan 2026
risks: Revenue growth chronically below peers for 3+ years
Net profit down 7% YoY in Q3FY26
JM Financial: no catalyst seen for re-rating
Consumer, retail, emerging markets remain soft
Nifty IT 1-year return: Wipro down 28.47% vs peers -15 to -21%

entry.rationale: Marginally above ideal entry — wait for pullback to ₹1,200–1,300; telecom turnaround under CEO Mohit Joshi needs 1–2 more quarters of validation

thesis.core_business: Telecom-focused large mid-cap IT. Dominant in BSS/OSS transformation for global telcos. Also present in manufacturing and BFSI. Undergoing restructuring under new CEO Mohit Joshi (joined 2023).
thesis.competitive_advantage: Deepest telecom domain expertise among Indian IT — unique positioning for 5G network transformation. GCC build-out for telcos. Mahindra Group governance backing.
growth_drivers: 5G network transformation mandates for global telcos
AI and network automation services for CSPs
New CEO restructuring — cost optimization driving margin recovery
Manufacturing and BFSI vertical diversification
risks: Telecom capex globally under pressure — core vertical headwinds
Turnaround still in progress — not yet demonstrated at scale
CapGemini/Ericsson competition in telecom IT space
Revenue growth lagged peers in FY24–25

companies.name: Mphasis
companies.category: Mid-Cap IT — BFSI Digital Transformation Specialist

entry.rationale: Approaching lower end of zone — BFSI concentration means strong recovery leverage when US financial services spend revives

thesis.core_business: Mid-cap IT services specialist in BFSI digital transformation. ~80% revenue from banking and financial services clients. DXC Technology (Blackstone-backed) is a major channel partner.
thesis.competitive_advantage: Deep BFSI domain knowledge — mortgage, lending, capital markets digitization. Direct exposure to US financial services recovery cycle. Low debt, strong cash generation.
growth_drivers: US BFSI discretionary spending recovery in H2 2026
Mortgage and lending digitization pipeline
Cloud-first financial services transformation
DXC channel partnership ramp-up
risks: ~80% BFSI concentration — high single-vertical exposure
DXC partnership dependency for deal flow
Jefferies downgraded to Hold (target ₹2,450)
US interest rate environment affecting BFSI capex decisions

financials.revenue_mix: Banking software licenses and services (~100% financial services)

price_data.note: Limited broker coverage — Oracle parent holds ~73% stake; limited free float reduces analyst attention

entry.rationale: Wait for 7–10% correction — best-in-class cash generation and ROE; niche banking software moat; limited free float means illiquidity premium

thesis.core_business: India-listed subsidiary of Oracle providing FLEXCUBE and Reveleus banking software to financial institutions globally. Near-monopoly in core banking software for emerging market banks.
thesis.competitive_advantage: FLEXCUBE core banking platform deeply embedded in 600+ banks globally — extremely high switching costs. Zero debt. 40%+ ROE. Oracle parentage provides credibility and product integration. License-led revenue provides predictability.
growth_drivers: Core banking modernization wave in emerging markets
FLEXCUBE upgrades and cloud migration of existing client base
Digital lending and payments module cross-sell
India domestic banking sector spending
risks: Oracle parent (73% stake) — limited free float, illiquidity
Growth slow vs pure-play IT services peers
Niche addressable market caps revenue growth potential
Expensive on absolute P/E at 30x for moderate growth
thesis.valuation_status: Slightly Rich — Worth It for Quality Moat

valuation_statuses: Slightly Rich — Worth It for Quality Moat
